description: Composes 5th-grade English Language Arts assessment items (Smarter Balanced · Grade 5 · Claim 1 (Reading) · Target 4: Reasoning & Evidence). A program authors an inline superset of tagged content for one literary passage — candidate inference claims and evidence sources — plus intended outcomes; the compiler selects the best-fitting content and assembles EBSR, Hot Text, or Short Text items. It selects and validates authored content; it does not generate it.

example_prompts.prompt: From a short story about a girl named Mara at a tide pool, write an EBSR item about what Mara cares about, standard rl-1.
example_prompts.notes: Two-part selected response: Part A inference statement, Part B supporting line; needs a supported claim plus at least 5 viable error-typed distractor claims (the item draws 3; a deeper pool gives selection real choice).

example_prompts.prompt: From the same tide-pool passage, also produce a short-text constructed-response item asking for an inference about Mara with evidence.
example_prompts.notes: Reuses the same pool; emits a prompt and a 0/1/2 rubric, no distractors.

example_prompts.prompt: Make a hot-text item where students click the sentences that show Mara's focus on the tide pool.
example_prompts.notes: Part B exposes every passage line as selectable; the correct claim's directly-supporting lines are the answer.

example_prompts.prompt: Add a faulty-reasoning distractor that mistakes Mara's quiet whisper for fear of her family.
example_prompts.notes: A distractor claim with error-type faulty-reasoning and a rationale; composition uses it for Part A error-type coverage.

example_prompts.prompt: Write an item about the narrator's point of view in a first-person diary entry, standard rl-6.
example_prompts.notes: Dimension point-of-view selects the matching supported claim and reports rl-6 alongside rl-1.

example_prompts.prompt: Compose an item comparing how two characters in the story react to the same event, standard rl-3.
example_prompts.notes: Dimension character-relationship; the companion standard rl-3 is emitted with rl-1.
